codeReview.summary: The feature correctly wires sign-only failures into SigningFailedModal and implements retry/dismiss behavior, but it does not render the peer-response field from real failure data as required.

issues.description: Timeout failures are rendered as a hard-coded "no peers responded" string (and non-timeout failures use other synthetic labels) rather than the real peer-response ratio from failure data. This can show incorrect information (e.g., some peers did respond before timeout), violating the feature requirement and VAL-OPS-006 expectation that description/round/peer-response/error values match the real failure observable.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-screen-worker skill requires Playwright + agent-browser verification for changed screens/modals, but this feature handoff only reports tsc + vitest and still marks followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/ui-screen-worker/SKILL.md:23,47-53 require agent-browser and playwright verification; mission handoff for this feature lists only tsc/vitest commands at lines 14-29.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Validation contract expects a peer-response ratio in SigningFailedModal, but the OperationFailure type currently lacks a peers-responded field, creating a contract/model gap that encourages heuristic UI text.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md:76-77 requires peer-response ratio from real failure payload; src/lib/bifrost/types.ts:370-375 defines OperationFailure without any peer-response count/ratio field.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking data-fidelity issue: peer-response text in SigningFailedModal is synthesized instead of sourced from real failure data, so the modal can present incorrect runtime context.
